children.text: Adyen
is a leading global
payment service provider (PSP)
and
acquirer
, headquartered in the Netherlands. The company offers a
unified commerce platform
that allows merchants to accept payments online, on mobile, and in-store through a single integration.
Adyen supports a vast range of
credit and debit cards, digital wallets, and local payment methods
across more than
200 countries and territories
. Its direct acquiring licenses with major card networks like
Visa, Mastercard, and American Express
help businesses streamline payment processing and settlement worldwide.
The platform also includes advanced
fraud prevention, risk management, recurring billing, and reporting tools
, designed for enterprises that require scalability and reliability. Adyen powers payments for some of the world’s largest brands in
eCommerce, retail, travel, SaaS, and marketplaces
By combining global reach with local expertise, Adyen enables merchants to optimize checkout experiences, expand internationally, and manage all payments in one place.

excerpt: Global payments technology platform for unified commerce (online, in-app, and in-store) with PSP and acquiring capabilities.

content_features.title: Unified commerce payments
content_features.description: Accept payments across online, in-app, and in-store channels with a single platform approach for reporting and operations.

content_features.title: Global payment method coverage
content_features.description: Supports cards, digital wallets, and a wide range of local payment methods to help merchants sell internationally.

content_features.title: Authorization optimization for higher approval rates
content_features.description: Use payment data and routing/processing options to reduce avoidable card declines and improve authorization success where possible.

content_features.title: Authentication and security controls
content_features.description: Supports security and authentication flows (for example, 3D Secure where applicable) to help balance conversion and risk.

content_features.title: Disputes and refunds workflows
content_features.description: Tools to help manage post-payment operations such as refunds and dispute handling, alongside payment visibility and reporting.

content_features.title: Enterprise reliability and scalability
content_features.description: Designed for high-volume merchants that need consistent performance across regions and channels.

tip: Track declines by reason and issuer country, then adjust your request data, routing, and retry logic based on the specific causes you see in your logs.

faq.question: Is Adyen a PSP or an acquirer?
faq.answer: Adyen is a payment service provider (PSP) and also offers acquiring in various markets, depending on the region and setup.

faq.question: What channels can I use Adyen for?
faq.answer: Adyen is commonly used for online payments, in-app/mobile payments, and in-store payments as part of a unified commerce setup.

faq.question: Which payment methods does Adyen support?
faq.answer: Adyen supports card payments, digital wallets, and many local payment methods. Specific availability depends on your account, country, and integration choices.

faq.question: How can I reduce card declines and improve approval rates with Adyen?
faq.answer: Review authorization and refusal reasons, validate the quality of the data you send (billing/shipping, shopper identifiers), and apply authentication (such as 3D Secure) where appropriate for your region and risk profile.

faq.question: Does Adyen support international expansion?
faq.answer: Adyen is a global platform and is commonly used for cross-border commerce, but supported countries, payment methods, and settlement options depend on your merchant entity and configuration.

faq.question: How are chargebacks and disputes handled?
faq.answer: Disputes are typically managed through Adyen’s customer area and/or API workflows, where you can review dispute details and submit responses according to scheme rules.

seo.seo_description: Adyen is a global payments technology platform offering PSP and acquiring capabilities for online, in-app, and in-store payments.
seo.keywords: Adyen, payments, PSP, acquirer, payment processing, unified commerce
